programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George Clarke explores hidden architectural gems in Finland, aided by master-craftsman Will Hardie. They begin at a classic Finnish lodge built on the edge of a frozen lake before venturing north into the arctic circle, hoping to catch a glimpse of the Northern Lights. Along the way, they visit collection of 60 tree houses, a mini city of igloos, and a cabin built in two weeks, before taking a husky ride to their final destination - a caravan on skis(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old lung cancer patient Marion is rushed to St George's with suspected sepsis. She was discharged from hospital the previous day after having an operation to remove liquid from her lung, but has deteriorated overnight. Eighty-three-year-Roy is brought into resus after falling off his roof at home, and doctors are concerned that he may have sustained internal injuries, while, five-year-old Laila has come to paediatrics with her mum Clarissa after cutting her head open at school(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of emergency services. programme.title: Guy Martin's Great Escape
programme.desc: Guy Martin wants to recreate the most iconic motorcycle stunt in movie history - the climax of the film The Great Escape, in which Steve McQueen's character Lieutenant Hilts steals a motorbike and attempts to jump two border fences into Switzerland. The original bike used in the film was a Triumph, and it's only right that Guy should convert and prep a modern Triumph Scrambler 1200 for his big stunt(n)

programme.title: Britain's Wildest Weather

Cameras also capture the sheer horror of being swept overboard by a monster wave in the middle of the night(n)

programme.title: My Grandparents' War
programme.desc: The actress discovers what happened to her grandfather, William, who served as a commanding officer in the Royal Navy during the Second World War. Kristin uncovers how he saved thousands of fellow soldiers at Dunkirk, and endured the horrors of the Russian Arctic convoys. Throughout the programme, Kristin meets the families of people her grandfather saved during the war, as well as veterans who served alongside him(n)

programme.title: Christmas at Chatsworth House
programme.desc: One-off documentary going behind the scenes of Chatsworth, one of England's most impressive stately homes, as the Duke, Duchess and their 800 staff brace themselves for an influx of 200,000 visitors over the 2019 festive season. Christmas is the most important event for the Derbyshire estate and crucial to its survival. In just five days, the house is transformed with 30 Christmas trees, 60,000 baubles, and a million fairy lights. But this year Derbyshire has been crippled by unprecedented flooding. Can family and staff rally to make this a season to be remembered?(n)

In Lewisham, the council's Rogue Landlord Unit raids one of their main streets, on the lookout for slum conditions, illegal overcrowding and criminal activities(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: The Yorkshire Air Ambulance crew is called to rescue a man trapped under a tractor that has plunged off a bridge and is in danger of sinking. Paramedic Paul is concerned his patient may go into cardiac arrest - and there's a real chance he may drown if firefighters cannot free him. The chopper lands on a farm to save a farmer who's been attacked by his own herd of cattle. Plus, on a road over the North York Moors, a wild deer runs out in front of a biker and leaves him with serious injuries(n)

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: Officers are called upon to restrain a man having a bad reaction to the 'zombie' drug Black Mamba. Cutting-edge documentary following the work of the Staffordshire Police force(n)

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Justin and Dawn's first job of the day starts with a call to a woman whose waters have broken, and former butcher Justin is in his element delivering the baby. Their next case is not so welcome though, as they attend a woman who has called 999 four times already that day and is one of a handful of prolific frequent callers staff across the ambulance service all know well(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than 200 RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK. The programme captures the unpredictable work of the vital rescue service, and provides an insight into the lives and motivations of the men and women who put their lives on the line to try and save strangers. In the first edition, a retired nursery teacher is in danger after attempting to rescue her two dogs when they became cut off by the rising tide. Meanwhile, in Wales, a volunteer is washed off a cliff after trying to rescue a concussed fisherman, and in Blackpool, three crew members' lives are in jeopardy(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping Feature. Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and his crack team of builders, plasterers and electricians come to the aid of Frank and Christine Burrows in Wigan, Greater Manchester. In 2012, the Burrows' 35-year-old son Mark retrained as a scaffolder to follow in his father's footsteps, but was paralysed as the result of an unprovoked attack that left him with severe brain damage. Mark now needs constant care, but the Burrows' family home is not yet fit for purpose, so Frank and Christine have been forced to take their son to a care home 15 miles away. With the help of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen, Nick's experts set about constructing a self-contained apartment alongside the Burrows' two-bedroom bungalow, which will provide Mark with the space, independence and security he will need to return home for good after spending two years away(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles comes to the aid of Liz and Jason Liversidge, a Hull couple in desperate need of help in future-proofing their family home. Not long after getting married and starting a family, Jason was diagnosed with both Fabrys disease, which causes a build-up of poisonous toxins in the body, and Motor Neurone Disease, which drastically reduced his life expectancy. The pair also discovered that Jason would have passed Fabrys disease on to his two daughters, which means that once her husband is gone, Liz will need all the help she can get in managing her children's condition. Nick joins forces with designer Oliver Heath and an army of volunteers to put together a home in which Jason can build meaningful memories with his girls, but one which also gives Liz the best chance of providing for their future needs(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Loughborough to meet a family of five, whose identical twin babies have been diagnosed as having a form of dwarfism, while at the age of 41, dad Mark is recovering from aggressive bowel and liver cancer. Now they are struggling for space. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their home, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

programme.title: Zigby

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: Stink argues with Tink and Wink so Zigby devises a plan to make them friends again(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Ground-breaking research suggests that changes to the bacteria living inside humans are making one in three people develop allergies. This programme follows an experiment involving members of two families to find out what it is in the modern world that's to blame. Mini-cameras, GPS units and the latest gene-sequencing technology help to uncover the impact of today's western lifestyle on their bacteria, why they are becoming allergic and what they can do in a bid to put a stop to the epidemic(n)

programme.title: Haunters of the Deep (1984)
programme.desc: An American businessman tries to reopen a Cornish tin mine, but the ghosts that haunt the tunnels have other ideas. Supernatural drama, starring Andrew Keir and Barbara Ewing(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath(n)

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: Two more contestants are introduced to the group in Sardinia, and Brendan Sheerin allows the group to visit a market following an Italian lesson. The contestants also try their hand at sea kayaking, and a couple face trouble at the vote(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: With electronic cigarettes increasing in popularity, presenter Michael Mosley considers whether they are safe to use. The benefits of caffeine in helping people stay alert are also explored, as well as alternatives to the chemical. Chris van Tulleken investigates whether household chores can legitimately count as exercise, while Saleyha Ahsan offers first aid tips to treat someone suffering from hypothermia. Plus, Gabriel Weston witnesses life-saving surgery that involves removing all the blood from a patient's body(n)

programme.title: The Truth About Meat
programme.desc: Greengrocer Chris Bavin sets out to find out whether certain claims about meat, linking it to cancer and heart disease, are justified. With the help of a team of scientists, he follows 40 volunteers on a study to find out whether there are any potential dangers, tests whether paying more for chicken makes it any better, and discovers a way to dramatically reduce the health risks associated with processed meats. Plus, an unlikely lean super-meat that will not break the bank(n)

programme.title: Miss World
programme.desc: Live coverage of the 69th annual contest from London, featuring contestants from across the world competing for the 2019 title(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a second home abroad(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Michael Mosley explores the latest research in genetics and neuroscience to find out what factors shape people's personalities and whether they can be changed. programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Bernard O'Mahoney explores the injustices suffered by those who have unintentionally surrounded themselves with criminals(n)

programme.title: Sparks and Embers (2015)
programme.desc: A French woman living in London ends her relationship with her boyfriend when she decides to return to her own country. On the day she is due to leave, they meet again for one last goodbye. Despite knowing their break-up is for the best, both find themselves tempted to stay together - but neither feels brave enough to say it. Romantic comedy, starring Kris Marshall and Annelise Hesme(n)

programme.title: Kids in Love (2016)
programme.desc: A student drifting through his gap year befriends a group of bohemians and gets drawn into their hedonistic world. He gives up everything to embrace their chaotic lifestyle, realising too late what he may have lost and that his new friends may not have his best interests at heart. Drama, starring Will Poulter and Alma Jodorowsky(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams investigates the scandal, theft and murder that flourished in the South East during the Second World War, as black market traders and criminals took advantage of Blitz conditions to make an easy living while their compatriots were risking life and limb in defence of the realm(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the contrasting stories of people responding to the traumas of the Second World War. While Kent student Daphne Baker set her heart on becoming a head teacher as her contribution to the war effort, conman Neville Heath embarked on a fraudulent spree preying on vulnerable women(n)

programme.title: The Stuarts in Exile
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which Dr Clare Jackson recalls Prince James Francis Stuart's audacious and long-running rebellion to reclaim his throne from the Hanoverian king George I. She tells the story of the Stuarts in exile and sheds new light on the political, military and cultural threat the Jacobites posed to the embryonic British state, polarising public opinion in the process(n)

programme.title: Hide and Seek (1972)
programme.desc: A boy runs away from a young offenders' institute to find his father, whom he discovers is planning a bank robbery. Crime drama, starring Peter Newby and Gary Kemp(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford(n)

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists continue their trip in Sardinia. A passenger with a fear of heights attempts a tree-top assault course, and one of the island's delicacies causes an upset stomach, before the travellers face more trouble at the vote(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Michael Mosley explores the latest research in genetics and neuroscience to find out what factors shape people's personalities and whether they can be changed. Michael tries two techniques in an attempt to make him worry less and become more of an optimist - with surprising results(n)

programme.title: The Purple Plain (1954)
programme.desc: A Canadian squadron leader's increasingly strange behaviour begins to worry the officers of an RAF station deep in the Burmese jungle. There is further concern when the unstable pilot crashes his plane miles from the base, forcing him to make an exhausting trek back to safety. Second World War drama, starring Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi and Maurice Denham(n)

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen (1960)
programme.desc: A former Army colonel, angry at his enforced retirement, sets out to exact an elaborate revenge on society in general. Recruiting seven other former servicemen who have turned to a life of crime, he plans a major bank robbery with military precision. Crime caper, starring Jack Hawkins, Nigel Patrick, Richard Attenborough, Roger Livesey and Bryan Forbes(n)

programme.title: The Hottest Day of Winter
programme.desc: An experimental visual reflection of the hottest winter day on record in the UK, which fell on February 26 2019(n)

programme.title: Capital Conversation

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: King Philip prepares to launch the Armada to avenge Mary's execution and Elizabeth pins her hopes on peace talks with Spain, but Drake's actions in Cadiz force her hand(n)

programme.title: Santa's Slave
programme.desc: Documentary about the controversial Dutch Christmas character of Zwarte Piet, Father Christmas' assistant, who is traditionally played by performers in blackface make-up(n)

programme.title: London Real
programme.desc: Inspirational speaker Brian Rose talks to Mark Manson about his book The Subtle Art of Not Giving a ****(n)

programme.title: Filming My Father: In Life and Death
programme.desc: In 2010, Steve Isaac was a fit 51-year-old father of three when he was diagnosed as having motor neurone disease and given two years to live. This film, to which his 22-year-old son Fraser contributed footage, documents the details of Steve's progressive condition and the decline in his health as muscles wasted away, as well as revealing the effect it has had on his family, who have been pushed to the brink by his desire to live at almost any cost. Four-and-a-half years on, Steve has defied medical expectation, but his only method of communicating now is blinking(n)

programme.title: Education Anglaise (1983)
programme.desc: An orphan is enrolled in a secretive school by her lecherous uncle, and discovers that the teachers run the establishment with strict discipline, while the students are all interested in expanding their sexual horizons. Comedy drama, starring Obaya Roberts and Brigitte Lahaie. In French(n)

programme.title: Great SAS Missions

Drama, starring Katrin Cartlidge, Frances Barber and James Fleet(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark and Amanda join Steve in a bid to prove an ambitious figure skater hired her former coach to murder a professional rival. Mystery, starring Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mark tries to catch the Casanova Killer, and loses his impartial view of the case when the murderer calls him. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The death of the prime suspect in the murder case suggests there is a copycat killer on the loose. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The mother of a model threatens to kill anyone who proposes to her daughter, and becomes the prime suspect when a private investigator is murdered. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars(n)

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse seeks advice from Dr Sloan when he suspects an improper procedure was the cause of a patient's death, and the pair become embroiled in a $2million life insurance fraud, in which a renowned surgeon is the prime suspect(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. McBride's commanding officer reveals the soldier was on a top-secret reconnaissance mission and asks Vance not to disclose the details to anyone. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons tries to get Gibbs to take responsibility for the many unnecessary deaths for which he holds the NCIS team accountable(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack(n)

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery

programme.desc: Purser Vicki Cox's boyfriend Nigel sneaks aboard a routine flight to ask for her hand in marriage. programme.title: The Hit List

This week's contestants are mother and daughter Tracey and Drea from High Wycombe, married couple Daniel and Rebecca from Aberdare, and brothers Karl and Ivan from Manchester. programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Watford at Anfield and Leicester City v Norwich City at the King Power Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's Reds held an extremely strong recent record over the Hornets, with their last three meetings on this ground finishing 5-0, 5-0 and 6-1. The home fans will have been hoping for more of the same to maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title. Plus, action from Burnley v Newcastle United at Turf Moor, Chelsea v Bournemouth at Stamford Bridge, Sheffield United v Aston Villa at Bramall Lane and Southampton v West Ham United at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Heist (2009)
programme.desc: Roger, a security guard working at an American museum, discovers several pieces from a collection he has defended for more than a decade are due to be sold to a gallery in Denmark. Chief among them is The Lonely Maiden, a painting he has grown to adore during his years of service. Determined to stop the sale from taking place, he enlists the aid of two like-minded guards in a desperate mission to steal the artworks they have come to love. Crime comedy, starring Christopher Walken, Morgan Freeman and William H Macy(n)

programme.title: Britain's Christmas Story
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson reveal the stories and legends behind some of Britain's festive traditions. Here they follow the story of Christmas on these shores from its origins, through the lavish partying of Elizabethan times, to the banning of the celebrations in the 17th-century. Along the way they discover that carols were once as much about dancing as singing, and find out why a holy site in Norfolk, known as England's Nazareth, once rivalled Jerusalem and Rome as a destination for pilgrims(n)

The Team of the Year, Coach of the Year, Lifetime Achievement, Overseas Sports Personality of the Year, Young Sports Personality of the Year, Unsung Hero and Helen Rollason awards will also be handed out, and there is live music from Lewis Capaldi and Emeli Sande(n)

She suggests that everyone go away for a weekend break together to neighbouring Co Kerry and try to learn to get along for Siobhan and the baby's sake, but no-one seems convinced this is a good idea(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Pointless co-host Alexander Armstrong takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with stand-up comedian Jon Richardson one of two guests joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Oscar-winners Judi Dench and Jennifer Hudson talk about the new film version of Cats, Hollywood stars Hugh Grant and Matthew McConaughey discuss their new action thriller The Gentlemen and everyone's favourite crooner Michael Buble also drops in for a chat. Plus, Coldplay offer an exclusive performance from their new album Everyday Life and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Join Lindsey, Richie and Henry for our big Blue Peter Christmas special! There's an amazing performance from the cast of hit musical The Boy in The Dress, a guest appearance from The Grinch and a spectacular 200 strong kids choir. Plus Christmas comes early for a group of fans who are awarded their Blue Peter badges in festive style; and our famous advent crown lights up the studio once again(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia reveals a harrowing past and Poppy takes over the show(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia and her friends hold the series wrap party in the flat(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team search for lethal beasts in Mexico. In the Yucatan Peninsula, he has an encounter with a deadly bull shark, then chases the fastest fish in the sea - the sailfish. Heading inland, Steve is awestruck by the hunting power of a million-strong army of bats as they take to the night sky and finally ventures into a dark cave of hanging serpents - the cold-blooded night snake that can catch a bat in mid-flight(n)

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Pirate Fairy (2014)
programme.desc: A rebellious fairy steals the magic pixie dust that grants her people the power of flight and runs away, becoming captain of a band of pirates. Tinker Bell and her friends set off to recover the dust, but succumb to a spell that switches their magical powers. Disney animated adventure, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Christina Hendricks and Tom Hiddleston(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the third heat. Eight teams battle it out to win a single place in the grand final, pitting robots Foxic, Expulsion, Heavy Metal, MR Speed Squared, Thor, Tauron, Chimera 2 and Concussion against each other in the arena. The show begins with two group battles, followed by a head-to-head between the two overall winners(n)

programme.title: Snow Babies
programme.desc: Documentary following all manner of baby animals born in some of the coldest and harshest places on Earth. Cameras follow the ups and downs of plucky emperor penguins, snow monkeys, polar bears, arctic foxes, reindeer and otters to find out what it takes to survive the first year of life in a world of snow and ice, with a little help from family and friends. Narrated by Caroline Quentin(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the first round of semi-finals week. The eight remaining contestants face two challenges, to be judged by Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing. Firstly, they must prepare a dish that captures the moment that triggered their own love of food. After this, they move on to the invention test, in which they must make a flawless vegan meal(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three remaining semi-finalists battle it out for a place in final, and are given the chance to work alongside one of the UK's most exciting chefs, Gareth Ward. First, the chefs are challenged to create a dish of their own design for Gareth to taste, inspired by his own cooking. The competitors will then take on the daunting task of joining Gareth's brigade, taking responsibility for two dishes each during a highly-pressurised service, before returning to the MasterChef kitchen to present a dish of their own design to the judges(n)

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella offers her tips for relaxed entertaining during the festive season, producing dishes including roast duck with orange, soy and ginger, garlic and parmesan mashed potato gratin and sour-sweet soused red cabbage with cranberries. There's also an exotic take on brussel sprouts, a sticky toffee pudding and chocolate pistachio meringue cookies(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three detached properties in St Albans - including a country lodge, a 1970s house, and an ultra-modern family home - are in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Robshaws time-travel through six decades of festive nostalgia, beginning by experiencing what the holiday season was like in the 1940s, '50s and '60s. A wartime Christmas has them embracing the make-do and mend spirit for presents and sitting down to ox heart for dinner, with a pudding made primarily of grated potato. The 1950s brings ham with tinned peaches, canapes, cocktails and the first televised Queen's speech, while youngest child Fred receives a 1960s gift of a chocolate smoking set. Giles Coren and Polly Russell present(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Part two of two. During their trip from the 1970s to the 1990s, the Robshaws discover how the race for a Christmas number one single was born. They also meet an EastEnders star who reminisces about the soap's tradition of festive misery, and examine Christmas gluttony in the 1990s(n)

programme.title: Nativity! (2009)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher is given the job of directing a nativity play and is determined to outdo a rival from a nearby private school. When he dishonestly claims his ex-girlfriend is a Hollywood producer and is coming to see the show with an eye on making a film, he finds himself becoming a local celebrity. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman and Ashley Jensen(n)

programme.title: Reindeer Family & Me
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan travels to Finnish Lapland to meet a family from the nomadic Sami culture, who rely on reindeer to help them travel across the frozen wilderness. He learns about the culture's traditional bond with the animals and picks up some tips on surviving during a harsh winter, before he and his reindeer companion undertake a gruelling journey to see the northern lights(n)

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Dinosaur
programme.desc: The story of how more than 220 dinosaur bones were found in the Argentinean desert, which were found to have come from a previously undiscovered species, which is the largest land dwelling animal known to have existed. David Attenborough visits the archaeological dig and a laboratory where the remains are being cleaned and analysed with lead scientist Dr Diego Pol and evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod, and meets animators, model-makers, paleontologists and anatomy experts who are working to reconstruct what the 37 metre-long creature would have looked like(n)

programme.title: Rod Stewart: Reel Stories
programme.desc: The singer talks to Dermot O'Leary about his life. From Abbey Road to the Royal Albert Hall and Motown to Maggie May, Dermot takes Rod back to his childhood cinema to relive moments from a career spanning six decades(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: The veteran rocker invites Alan Yentob to his LA home to conduct an interview, in which Stewart looks back on a career spanning almost 50 years. He discusses working with acts like the Steampacket and Jeff Beck in the 1960s and his time with the Faces, whose offstage antics became legendary - as did the frontman's affairs with a string of famous women. His solo career propelled him to superstardom with songs such as Maggie May and Sailing, and while the 1980s proved to be less successful, his constant reinvention has given him a lasting career. Having more recently become known as a singer of American standards, he talks about recording Time, his first album of new material in almost 20 years(n)

programme.title: Rod Stewart Live at Hyde Park
programme.desc: A concert by the singer that closed Radio 2's annual Festival in a Day, held in September 2015 in London's Hyde Park. Featuring a selection of hits from his back catalogue including Angel, In a Broken Dream, The Killing of Georgie (Part I and II) as well as Faces classics including Ooh La La and blues standard Rollin' and Tumblin'(n)

programme.title: Just Jim (2015)
programme.desc: A Welsh teenager leads a lonely life after being rejected by his only friend. He becomes fascinated by a mysterious American who moves in next door, and the new arrival offers him some advice on how to be cool. The youngster starts to develop the life he had always longed for, but begins to wonder if he was happier the way he used to be. Comedy drama, starring Emile Hirsch and Craig Roberts(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: As the monks crowd around Malachia's body, William questions who killed this man since Bernardo had cleverly found and confined the murderer of the other monks. A seemingly unconcerned Bernardo retorts that he had not claimed to have confined the law or the criminals at large in the abbey and warns that only the pope will decide the fate of the Franciscans. William tells Adso that Bernardo with burn Remigio and his pyre will illuminate the encounter between Michele of Cesena - minister general of the Franciscans - and the pope. John Turturro and Rupert Everett star(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Hollyoaks stars Will Mellor and Nick Pickard hunt for bargains in north west England, assisted by experts Paul Laidlaw and Steph Connell. Along the way, they uncover the unusual history of Merseyside village Port Sunlight and learn about glass-making, before bringing their finds - a pocket watch and a ship's porthole - to Greenwich to sell at auction(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton, Danny Clarke and the team transform a Manchester back garden into a time travel-themed space for a Doctor Who fan(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: The horticultural tour continues with a trip to South Africa, one of the greenest zones in the world. In Cape Town, Monty Don visits the renowned Kirstenbosch Botanic Gardens, where he encounters the country's national plant, the king protea, and discovers an abundance of plant life in the Drakensberg mountains(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team are in Wiltshire where John Craven is in a wood that's ravaged with ash dieback, and Anita Rani meets a craftsman who's keeping the ancient art of bee skep making alive. Steve Brown pays a visit to one of the country's top wood engravers, and Adam Henson is in Lincolnshire seeing for himself the impact of the recent devastating floods(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt looks back at highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef concludes his tour with trips to the Languedoc and Provence. In the border town of Uzes, Rick samples salt cod and marvels at a colourful array of French patisserie - selecting a seasonal fig tart to have with his morning coffee. He moves on to the harbour town of Cassis to try North African lamb chorba cooked by a local fisherman, while back in his country kitchen, he prepares a comfit tomato and aubergine tart tatin(n)

programme.title: Tron: Legacy (2010)
programme.desc: Ground-breaking computer genius Kevin Flynn disappears without trace. Years later, his son Sam goes looking for him, and is transported into a computer-generated world where he is forced to compete in deadly gladiatorial combat by an evil duplicate of his father plotting to wage war on the real world. Sci-fi adventure, with Jeff Bridges and Garrett Hedlund(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The last semi-final sees the remaining hopefuls head to Ollie Dabbous's new London restaurant Hide, where they receive a masterclass from the Michelin-starred chef in making his beef tartare. He then asks them to create a dish from the restaurant's select larder before they are each given responsibility for one of the dishes during a packed service. Back at the studio, a final test awaits in which the hopefuls must cook two dishes for Gregg Wallace, Marcus Wareing and Monica Galetti, who decide who will go through to next week's finals(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create dishes for unexpected guests, with impressionist Jon Culshaw joining them to make a last-minute Christmas cake, and orange and honey glazed salmon(n)

programme.title: Nativity 2: Danger in the Manger! (2012)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher enters his class in a Song for Christmas competition, and ends up having to lead the busload of excitable kids - as well as his irresponsible teaching assistant - on a disastrous trip to Wales for the event. The journey also leads to an unwanted confrontation with his high-flying twin brother. Comedy sequel, starring David Tennant, Marc Wootton, Joanna Page and Jessica Hynes(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Festive edition from 2016 in which Gregg Wallace, Cherry Healey and Ruth Goodman visit places where traditional Christmas items roll off the production line. Gregg goes to Britain's biggest cake factory, where 180 million mince pies are produced each year, and pitches in on the yule log conveyor belt. Cherry finds out where tinsel and wrapping paper are made and Ruth learns the history of Christmas crackers(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness get to grips with a disused hearse, and give it a new lease of life as the ultimate family car. On the track, Chris puts the Dallara Stradale through its paces, while royal couple Zara and Mike Tindall go head-to-head in Star in a Reasonably Fast Car(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A festive edition of the business ideas contest, with Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assessing the profit-making potential of ideas perfect for the Christmas season. An inventor introduces his innovative new form of Christmas tree stand, while other entrepreneurs pitch their ideas for a range of sweets and a puzzling family game. Finally, the creator of a personalised comic-book realises he must make a big personal decision if he is to secure funding. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: Borg McEnroe (2017)
programme.desc: During the summer of 1980, acclaimed Swedish tennis player Bjorn Borg is training for an unprecedented fifth consecutive Wimbledon men's singles title, with only one thing standing in his way - the brash and abrasive young American John McEnroe. A rivalry develops between the pair, culminating in one of the greatest matches of all time in that year's final. Biopic, starring Sverrir Gudnason, Shia LaBeouf and Stellan Skarsgard(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Alice Levine, Alan Carr and Phill Jupitus join Alan Davies in answering questions from Sandi Toksvig on the subject of Quests(n)

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: The Ivor Novello Award and Brit Award-winner performs at the BBC Radio Theatre in London, following his global stadium tour. Having released his history-making third studio album Divide in March 2017, Ed has continued to dominate charts worldwide since its release and has solidified his status as one of the world's most talented modern-day performers(n)

programme.title: Seamus Heaney and the Music of What Happens
programme.desc: Documentary about the life and work of Irish poet Seamus Heaney, with his widow Marie and children Michael, Christopher and Catherine talking about their family life and reading some of the poems he wrote for them. His surviving brothers Hugh, Charlie, Colum and Dan remember their childhood and the shared experiences that inspired many of his finest poems(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three terraces in Cardiff are in the spotlight, featuring an Edwardian property with a modern update, one designed with a nod to the peaceful, and a revamped Victorian house(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is confined to a psychiatric unit, and refuses to accept that she has a problem. However, a visit from Fletch, Sacha and Elliot convinces her that things have to change for Emma's sake. Chloe receives some life-changing news, and decides that she has to tell Evan's sister the truth about her dead brother. Zav and Cameron try to save the life of a girl who has been stabbed, only to discover a disturbing truth behind the attack(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Three treasured family heirlooms are brought back to life as the team takes on a broken reel-to-reel tape recording machine, a costume that once belonged to a 1950s strong woman and a wind-up car. Presented by Jay Blades(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Crews tend to a 95-year-old Navy veteran as he is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. He's diabetic, dehydrated and has suspected acute pancreatitis. Meanwhile. a man has his holiday interrupted with kidney problems. Narrated by Kevin Whately(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Cocker Spaniel Tilly has been in labour for 12 hours and is struggling to give birth. It's a potentially life-threatening situation, so vet Maurice King decides to perform an urgent C-section to deliver a valuable litter of pups. A young jackdaw is brought into the clinic unable to fly. The vets rehome him with a local woman who has Asperger's syndrome, who has a particular affinity with animals. At the Downpatrick clinic Dexter - a therapy dog at the local hospital - is brought in to see vet Rachael Frew. Dexter has had cancerous lumps on his skin before and more have recently appeared(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: The Best Christmas Food Ever
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins chefs Paul Ainsworth and Catherine Fulvio, who serve up roast turkey with a colourful roast butternut squash and feta salad and date and cranberry relish(n)

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk presents as Anna Haugh prepares a dish served at a royal wedding and Polly Russell goes to Gloucester to trace the history of an ancient royal delicacy(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made by actor Noel Coward, featuring archive interview clips showing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Brief Encounter (1945)
programme.desc: A suburban housewife meets a married doctor by chance in a railway station waiting room. Later, their paths cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. David Lean's romantic drama, with Celia Johnson, Trevor Howard and Cyril Raymond(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King are joined by actress Sally Lindsay to create pici verde pasta with Christmas meatballs, beetroot and goats' cheese risotto, and a turkey tortilla soup(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Anita Manning are at Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where they assess items including a selection of golf memorabilia and a trio of Moorcroft ceramics(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Lolly Adefope, Dan Walker, Sarah Greene and Miles Jupp go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as Formula 1 fans the Motorheads take on Doctor Who aficionados the Time Ladies The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Monopoly playing pieces, Spectators at the US Masters, Contestants on Mastermind, and The rules of cricket(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace, Cherry Healey and Ruth Goodman explore the fascinating factory processes and surprising history behind the nation's favourite festive treats. Gregg follows 24 hours of production at a factory in Oldham, Greater Manchester, which will be making two million Christmas cakes for Marks and Spencer, while Cherry visits Britain's largest marzipan manufacturer as well as a sprout farm. Ruth investigates how the UK's early industrial heritage inspired Charles Dickens to write A Christmas Carol(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Endeavours take on the 007s for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one question asking what comes next in the sequence 8. King Kong, 7. Carol Cleveland, 6. Extrasensory perception(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Responsible Child
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following the story of Ray, a boy on the cusp of adolescence who is arrested along with his adult brother Nathan for the murder of his mum's abusive partner. With the age of criminal responsibility set at 10, Ray stands trial for murder in an adult courtroom. The film follows both the events leading up to the crime, and the boy's experience of the trial. Starring Billy Barratt, Michelle Fairley, Tom Burke and James Tarpey(n)

programme.title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019
programme.desc: Philomena Cunk is joined by guests to look back at the year. Comedy, starring Diane Morgan(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Love & Friendship (2016)
programme.desc: A widow trying to escape scandalous rumours resolves to find suitable husbands for herself and her daughter. Period comedy, starring Kate Beckinsale and Chloe Sevigny(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe sifts through the Top of the Pops archives to present a selection of festive performances, including songs by Slade, the Pogues, Elton John, Take That, Coldplay and Abba(n)

programme.title: What We Were Watching: Christmas 1988
programme.desc: Grace Dent looks at the television shows that Britain's viewers were watching over the Christmas period of 1988, focussing on that week's TV schedules to explore how TV offerings change over the festive season(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Julian has gone missing from the foster home and Harry is convinced he has been abducted. When police search Mosswood, Vera is adamant that she is not responsible. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Harry discovers that an eyewitness saw a man arguing with Marin outside the Five Nations motel room, but that Julian was not with her. Brick calls Harry with an important lead that was found during the raid on Mosswood. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Bob Monkhouse
programme.desc: The story of the comedian and game show host's life and career, told through the archive of films, TV shows, letters and memorabilia that he left behind after his death in 2003. Contributors include Ronnie Corbett, Barry Cryer, Michael Grade and Lenny Henry(n)

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: The singer-songwriter performs an intimate concert at the Maida Vale Studios in London. Featuring new songs from Still on My Mind, along with hits from No Angel and Life for Rent(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: In the wake of the First World War, former sapper Thomas Shelby and his brothers return from the trenches to their hometown of Birmingham, where their family runs a feared crime ring - making money from illegal betting, protection and the black market. Thomas recognises an opportunity to move up in the world when a crate of guns goes missing from an arms factory, but government minister Winston Churchill sends in a ruthless police chief from Belfast to impose order on the increasingly lawless city and recover the weapons. First episode of the drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Chief Inspector Campbell carries out a violent raid on the gang's territory and demands a meeting with Thomas, who is later sent a bullet with his name on it after picking a fight with a gypsy family at a country fair. Meanwhile, Aunt Polly is shocked to discover Ada is pregnant, and when Thomas discovers his former war comrade Freddie Thorne is the father, he devises a plan to get his old friend out of town. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Two men claiming to be IRA sympathisers approach Thomas in the Garrison and offer to buy the missing crate of guns they have heard he has in his possession. The gang leader also goes to Cheltenham races with Grace, where he aims to persuade kingpin Billy Kimber to make the Peaky Blinders part of his operation, while Ada secretly marries communist agitator Freddie Thorne. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Everton v Arsenal at Walton Hall Park, Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at the Academy Stadium and Liverpool v Chelsea at Prenton Park(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Biologist Ben Garrod examines the evolution of skin - the largest organ in any creature's body - across all species of the animal kingdom, beginning by looking at how that of vertebrates has allowed them to conquer the planet.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Great Barrier Reef with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: The naturalist travels with a specialist dive team looking for signs of hope that the Great Barrier Reef can survive the threat of global warming. Invited on the journey by award-winning underwater cinematographer and marine biologist Richard Fitzpatrick, Iolo dives deep beneath the tranquil surface of the coral sea to discover what state this natural wonder is in(n)

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. The second leg of Simon's journey begins at the Giant's Causeway, before visiting a kelp farm on Rathlin Island. He heads to the Dark Hedges in Co Antrim, one of the filming locations from Game of Thrones, and visits Belfast for the July 12 parades, discovering how the event has divided the city. He also learns more about Oliver Cromwell's influence on Ireland, attends the Gaze film festival, and meets monkeys at an animal sanctuary in the Wicklow mountains(n)

programme.title: Norman Wisdom: His Story
programme.desc: The life and career of the comedy actor, told by those who knew him well, from his son, daughter and daughter-in-law to famous friends including Stephen Frears, Ricky Tomlinson, Leslie Phillips, Honor Blackman and Vera Lynn(n)

programme.title: Northern Ballet's Victoria
programme.desc: Premiered on March 9, 2019, at Sadler's Wells, London, and coinciding with the year that marks the 200th anniversary of Queen Victoria's birth, Northern Ballet's Victoria, choreographed by Cathy Marston, captures the passions, tragedies and fierce devotion of the life of one of Britain's most memorable monarchs(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: The war with the Lees escalates when they ransack the Shelbys' betting shop, while John reveals he intends to remarry, but his choice of bride does not go down well with his family. Chief Inspector Campbell puts more pressure on Thomas to deliver Freddie Thorne and the crate of stolen guns to him, while Grace begins to glean more information about the Peaky Blinders' illegal operations(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas deals with an IRA chief who has come to Small Heath to avenge his cousin's death. Meanwhile, Campbell gets closer to finding the stolen guns and Grace has to decide whether her loyalties lie with the inspector or with Thomas. Period gangster drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Peaky Blinders
programme.desc: Thomas prepares to oust Billy Kimber at Worcester Racecourse, but secrets are revealed that force the Shelbys to face up to the problems that have divided them. Meanwhile, Campbell unleashes one last plan to destroy the Peaky Blinders and Grace finds herself with an impossible decision to make. Drama, starring Cillian Murphy and Sam Neill(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams made up of distinguished graduates of Sheffield and Manchester compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. Among those facing Jeremy Paxman's questions are politician David Blunkett and political commentator Paul Mason for Sheffield, with journalist David Aaronovitch and Watchdog presenter Matt Allwright representing Manchester(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Skin
programme.desc: Ben Garrod explores how skin has evolved to enable animals to move in a range of habitats, revealing how adaptations allow birds to fly, sharks to speed through water and sugar gliders to take to the air.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Juliet Stevenson narrates a documentary filmed over the course of a year that tells the stories of the animals and people inhabiting Icleand. An Arctic fox family ekes out a clifftop living, an eider farmer has his hands full with the arrival of a host of ducklings and Viking horsemen prepare to saddle up for the autumn round-up. However, nature's clock is ticking and the constant volcanic threat eventually boils over with one of the island's biggest eruptions in more than 200 years(n)

programme.title: Toy Empire: The British Force Behind Star Wars Toys
programme.desc: David Whiteley reveals how an unassuming band of designers and factory workers in Coalville, Leicestershire produced the Star Wars action figures that were to define a generation. David meets the Palitoy employees workers who succeeded in getting the toys on the shelves against all the odds, and the people whose collections of them are now worth eye-watering sums(n)

programme.title: The Galaxy Britain Built: The British Force Behind Star Wars
programme.desc: David Whiteley celebrates the unsung British heroes behind the first film in the sci-fi franchise, including the contribution of the London Symphony Orchestra of the London Symphony Orchestra. He also meets Ann Skinner, who was in charge of continuity, who reveals how she helped Alec Guinness with one of the most famous speeches in Star Wars(n)

programme.title: Score: Cinema's Greatest Soundtracks

Jurgen Klopp's Reds held an extremely strong recent record over the Hornets, with their last three meetings on this ground finishing 5-0, 5-0 and 6-1. The home fans will have been hoping for more of the same to maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title. Plus, action from Burnley v Newcastle United at Turf Moor, Chelsea v Bournemouth at Stamford Bridge, Sheffield United v Aston Villa at Bramall Lane and Southampton v West Ham United at St Mary's Stadium(n)

Chief among them is The Lonely Maiden, a painting he has grown to adore during his years of service. Determined to stop the sale from taking place, he enlists the aid of two like-minded guards in a desperate mission to steal the artworks they have come to love. Crime comedy, starring Christopher Walken, Morgan Freeman and William H Macy(n)

programme.title: Britain's Christmas Story
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson reveal the stories and legends behind some of Britain's festive traditions. Here they follow the story of Christmas on these shores from its origins, through the lavish partying of Elizabethan times, to the banning of the celebrations in the 17th-century. Along the way they discover that carols were once as much about dancing as singing, and find out why a holy site in Norfolk, known as England's Nazareth, once rivalled Jerusalem and Rome as a destination for pilgrims(n)

programme.desc: Claire Skinner presents an appeal on behalf of the charity Kids(n)

programme.title: The Penguins of Madagascar
programme.desc: Skipper, Kowalski, Private and Rico embark on an adventure on Christmas Eve. Animated film spin-off of the popular films, featuring the voices of John DiMaggio and Tom McGrath(n)

programme.title: Madagascar 3: Europe's Most Wanted (2012)
programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals continue their quest to get back to their New York home. Having made it to Monte Carlo, they join a circus on a European tour as a means of travelling undetected - but an animal control agent is in hot pursuit. Animated comedy, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in York, helping a couple who have spent two years painstakingly renovating their terraced home. With a very, very small garden, full of crazy paving and building materials, Charlie and the boys compete to give it a £5,000k facelift, turning it into a luxurious haven to relax and entertain in. Along the way, they have a host of ideas and tips for making the most of small spaces and shady corners(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: England midfielder Jill Scott, Olympic Taekwondo silver medallist Lutalo Muhammad, rugby league great Kevin Sinfield and Great Britain 400m runner Emily Diamond join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Halloween presents one of the last big sales opportunities of the year, so the pressure is on Emma and Martin from the produce team, battling the wet weather to ensure this year's pumpkin crop will deliver the one million required for stores around the country. In the Locksbottom branch in Kent, Halloween could end up being a bit of a nightmare. The store has a low customer satisfaction rating, so manager Mij is on a mission to retrain and re-invigorate his young team in preparation for the big pumpkin push. Meanwhile, Laura and Donna from the communications team head to Romania for the filming of Sainsbury's Dickensian-themed Christmas advert. How will it stand up against the commercials produced by their rivals?(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Margherita Taylor and Steve Brown are in Buckinghamshire, where Matt is with a group of schoolchildren making their very first visit to a farm. Margherita meets the people working hard to preserve some of the UK's last box trees and finds out what's at stake for those that use it to make fine woodwind instruments, while Steve heads to Tiggywinkles wildlife hospital, where he helps return a couple of barn owls to the wild(n)

programme.title: Sports Personality of the Year
programme.desc: Gabby Logan, Clare Balding and Gary Lineker present this year's ceremony live from P&J Live Arena in Aberdeen. Geraint Thomas was presented the prestigious main award in 2018, and the nominees this time around are cricketer Ben Stokes, sprinter Dina Asher-Smith, Formula One star Lewis Hamilton, heptathlete Katarina Johnson-Thompson, Wales rugby union legend Alun Wyn Jones and Manchester City and England forward Raheem Sterling. The Team of the Year, Coach of the Year, Lifetime Achievement, Overseas Sports Personality of the Year, Young Sports Personality of the Year, Unsung Hero and Helen Rollason awards will also be handed out, and there is live music from Lewis Capaldi and Emeli Sande(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Separated from her friends, Lyra must use all of her methods of deception to thwart a formidable foe. Meanwhile, Mrs Coulter plots her next move. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's epic fantasy, starring Dafne Keen and Ruth Wilson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Arsenal v Manchester City at Etihad Stadium, as the Citizens looked to extend a run of five consecutive victories over the Gunners. Plus, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Tottenham Hotspur at Molineux, Manchester United v Everton at Old Trafford, and a round-up of yesterday's goals(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Everton v Arsenal at Walton Hall Park, Manchester City v Brighton & Hove Albion at the Academy Stadium and Liverpool v Chelsea at Prenton Park(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Meet the Street at Christmas
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser goes into communities around Wales and creates choirs for people who feel isolated, beginning by setting one up in a rural area. Mark meets Dossie, a villager who now lives alone after her husband passed away, and Ella, who has recently moved back with her parents after university and is struggling to make new friends. Can he pull a group together not only to put on a performance of their lifetimes, but to help them make lasting connections with a real impact?(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell revisits a detached house in Gillingham, Kent, she first saw in 2017. Plus, a semi-detached in Walsall, West Midlands, and an end terrace in Stoke-on-Trent(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A specialist surgeon travels to Shetland to carry out a complex operation, while a Fair Isle man hopes to get the all-clear after surgery to find out if cancer has been kept at bay(n)

programme.title: Only Foals and Horses
programme.desc: Vets Lisa Durham and Philippa Hughes help to artificially inseminate a precious Arabian mare. Philippa also has to create tiny plastic shoes to help one of her smallest patients - a Shetland pony foal - cure its bandy legs, and the clinic team works hard in the operating theatre to treat a deadly infection threatening the life of a much-loved family pet(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for Christmas bargains at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while presenter Charlie Ross visits York Theatre Royal to learn about the history of pantomime(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma struggles to make sense of what has happened, while Ruhma has surprise news for a pregnant woman in police custody(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Following reports of Flambeau's death, the clerical sleuth races to protect a crown that contains a nail from the crucifixion of Christ. Mark Williams stars(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads to South Wales with a couple who are looking for a country home after three decades of military housing, and have a budget of £430,000 to spend. Away from the property search, Jules dons his own army fatigues to discover how the beautiful Welsh wilderness prepares soldiers for battle(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie, David and Harry face a challenge in Liphook, Hampshire, where a couple have a big garden and a daughter with her own ideas on what makes the perfect outdoor space(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Lucia Scalisi tries to mend a serious tear in a portrait, William Kirk and Steven Fletcher join forces to fix a walking stick and Brenton West tackles a seaside telescope(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For the past five years, a radical new church has been promising hundreds of young people an escape from gangs and crime and a pathway to salvation through prosperity and success. SPAC Nation, the Salvation Proclaimers Anointed Church, holds services in hotels, conference halls and football grounds and leader Tobi Adegboyega has been courted by politicians and the media for his work in tackling gang violence. But Panorama has discovered how the church's outward show of concern for young people may hide a less than charitable appetite for money. Reporter Greg McKenzie investigates its fundraising activities and speaks to young worshippers who say they've been forced into debt whilst church leaders enjoy lavish lifestyles(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil is sure Sharon and Jack are having an affair and sets out for revenge, despite Ben's efforts to convince him otherwise. Martin is on a high following another job with Tubbs, only for Sonia to bring him down when she quizzes him about his sudden wealth. Mick is frustrated when Ollie does not land a main role in the school nativity so he plans his own version - but Linda, Tina and Shirley make him realise it will not be quite so easy. Jay entrusts Stuart to manage the undertaker's(n)

programme.title: A Berry Royal Christmas
programme.desc: The Duke and Duchess of Cambridge invite Mary to accompany them on four special visits to charities they support, meeting staff who work throughout the festive season. Mary also prepare a feast for a Christmas party hosted by the royal couple for the volunteers and gets help preparing the dishes from special guests including former Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain - and the Duke and Duchess themselves. Throughout the programme Mary also cooks some of her favourite Christmas recipes for viewers to try out at home(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen looks through the archives to see how Wales has celebrated the festive season over the past 50 years. Featuring a grandmother who had her own festive grotto, a Christmas baby from 1971, a seasonal food hamper being sent to the Skerrie Islanders and a bus driver who keeps his passengers in the mood by wearing a tree on his head(n)

programme.title: Elis James: Funny Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up explores the influence of Wales' different performance traditions on the nation's comedy and on its biggest stars. He discovers the lasting legacy of the popular 70s double act Ryan and Ronnie on some of today's biggest performers, finds out how the folk club scene helped produce Max Boyce and sees how Rhod Gilbert has inspired a new generation of performers - including the latest winner of the Edinburgh comedy award Jordan Brookes(n)

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The baby is due soon and Siobhan is upset with the nonstop arguing of both families, so Orla tries to come up with a solution. She suggests that everyone go away for a weekend break together to neighbouring Co Kerry and try to learn to get along for Siobhan and the baby's sake, but no-one seems convinced this is a good idea(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The festive season arrives once again in the Brown household, and Father Damian has organised a Christmas decoration contest to support the local football team. Agnes is keen to enter, especially as it might mean getting one over her old nemesis, Hillary Nicholson, but is soon distracted by her new 'wi-fi assistant' and quite what to do with it(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Sabrina Ghayour and David Munoz, while guest Alice Levine faces her food heaven or hell. Olly Smith selects the wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Calvin, Lesley, Don and Suzanne - colleagues from The Old Mart Resource Centre in Maud, Aberdeenshire - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Liverpool v Watford at Anfield in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: Doddie Weir: One More Try
programme.desc: In 2016, Scottish rugby legend Doddie Weir was diagnosed with motor neurone disease. Doctors said he would be in a wheelchair within a year, but instead Doddie has battled the disease and is on a mission to help find a cure. Fellow player John Beattie has followed Doddie and his family over the past two years, gaining a unique insight into the life and mission of the man behind the famous tartan suit(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a £300,000 budget find a country character property that gives them community on their doorstep and a crash pad for their grandchildren to stay during the holidays. Along the way, the presenter meets a Devon stovemaker and finds out the practical and environmental benefits of this popular country home interior feature(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: A keeper at Longleat House and Safari Park discovers a concerning abscess on one of her red pandas. After antibiotics and painkillers fail to help the growth, Koda needs an urgent visit to the vet for an operation. Presented by Ben Fogle and Kate Humble(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Sports Personality of the Year edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Ama Agbeze, Greg Rusedski, Eddie Izzard, Hannah Cockcroft, Rebecca Adlington, Michael O'Neill, Jayne Torvill, Robin Cousins and Geraint Thomas(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the high-tempo, music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a potential £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are mother and daughter Tracey and Drea from High Wycombe, married couple Daniel and Rebecca from Aberdare, and brothers Karl and Ivan from Manchester. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise hits from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the final of the pro-celebrity contest, which sees the remaining couples - Kelvin Fletcher and Oti Mabuse, Emma Barton and Anton Du Beke, and Karim Zeroual and Amy Dowden - perform a routine of their own choosing, one set by the judges and one last show dance. As usual Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse will be reviewing their efforts, but only the viewers' votes will count to determine the winner. There is also a performance by pop superstar Taylor Swift(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Ellie Goulding hands Michael her phone for Send To All and there is a performance by tenor Andrea Bocelli. Plus, two brothers from Coventry visit a relaxing spa, but get a shock when they find themselves on stage at the London Palladium as they become the Unexpected Stars of the Show(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Liverpool v Watford at Anfield and Leicester City v Norwich City at the King Power Stadium. Jurgen Klopp's Reds held an extremely strong recent record over the Hornets, with their last three meetings on this ground finishing 5-0, 5-0 and 6-1. The home fans will have been hoping for more of the same to maintain their push for a first-ever Premier League title. Plus, action from Burnley v Newcastle United at Turf Moor, Chelsea v Bournemouth at Stamford Bridge, Sheffield United v Aston Villa at Bramall Lane and Southampton v West Ham United at St Mary's Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's AFC game - Baltimore Ravens v New York Jets at M&T Bank Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Heist (2009)
programme.desc: Roger, a security guard working at an American museum, discovers several pieces from a collection he has defended for more than a decade are due to be sold to a gallery in Denmark. Chief among them is The Lonely Maiden, a painting he has grown to adore during his years of service. Determined to stop the sale from taking place, he enlists the aid of two like-minded guards in a desperate mission to steal the artworks they have come to love. Crime comedy, starring Christopher Walken, Morgan Freeman and William H Macy(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Scotland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, with reports from around Scotland(n)

programme.title: Britain's Christmas Story
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gareth Malone and Karen Gibson reveal the stories and legends behind some of Britain's festive traditions. Here they follow the story of Christmas on these shores from its origins, through the lavish partying of Elizabethan times, to the banning of the celebrations in the 17th-century. Along the way they discover that carols were once as much about dancing as singing, and find out why a holy site in Norfolk, known as England's Nazareth, once rivalled Jerusalem and Rome as a destination for pilgrims(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A huge 1930s house in Kidderminster in Worcestershire and a purpose-built flat in a 1980s block in Wembley in north London are sold at auction(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion challenges two teams to scour an antiques fair in Dorking, Surrey, to find items to sell at auction, with Caroline Hawley and David Harper offering advice(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
